Per EPA AIM Act Technology Transitions rule effective January 1, 2025, manufacture/import of new R-410A residential AC and heat pump equipment is prohibited. Verify that equipment is pre-2025 manufactured inventory and confirm long-term warranty/service support availability.",
      "AFUE 80% is below current federal minimum efficiency standard (95% AFUE for new gas furnaces as of 2024). This represents an older or lower-efficiency unit.",
      "No Manual J load calculation mentioned for full system replacement.",

      "Labor costs not itemized separately from equipment total."

    "summary": "Full heat pump system replacement with Carrier 16 SEER2 equipment and 80% AFUE furnace for $9,200 in Charlotte, NC. Installation includes permit. However, multiple concerns reduce value: R-410A refrigerant is non-compliant with EPA rules effective 2025-2026 unless pre-2025 stock is confirmed; 80% AFUE furnace is below current federal minimum; warranty is limited to 1 year (unusually short); and no load calculation is documented. Verify refrigerant compliance, furnace efficiency standards, and request extended warranty details before accepting this quote.",

  "summary_for_llm": "Total: $9,200.\nSystem: heat_pump.\nBrand: Carrier.\nSEER: 16.\nAFUE: 80%.\nTonnage: 2.5 tons.\nRefrigerant: R-410A.\nParts warranty: 1 year.\nLabor warranty: 1 year workmanship.\n\n6 red flag(s):\n  - Quote is 10% above the top of the expected price range ($8,353)\n  - R-410A refrigerant specified for new installation. Per EPA AIM Act Technology Transitions rule effective January 1, 2025, manufacture/import of new R-410A residential AC and heat pump equipment is prohibited. Verify that equipment is pre-2025 manufactured inventory and confirm long-term warranty/service support availability.\n  - AFUE 80% is below current federal minimum efficiency standard (95% AFUE for new gas furnaces as of 2024). This represents an older or lower-efficiency unit.\n  - No Manual J load calculation mentioned for full system replacement.\n  - Warranty limited to 1 year on both parts and labor — significantly below industry standard (typically 5-10 years parts, 2-5 years labor for new installations).\n  - Labor costs not itemized separately from equipment total.\n\nSummary: Full heat pump system replacement with Carrier 16 SEER2 equipment and 80% AFUE furnace for $9,200 in Charlotte, NC. Installation includes permit. However, multiple concerns reduce value: R-410A refrigerant is non-compliant with EPA rules effective 2025-2026 unless pre-2025 stock is confirmed; 80% AFUE furnace is below current federal minimum; warranty is limited to 1 year (unusually short); and no load calculation is documented. Verify refrigerant compliance, furnace efficiency standards, and request extended warranty details before accepting this quote."
